_attribution: LA FORJA team (Manuel Peña Zúñiga). Original item authored by the team.
stem: In a right triangle, two of the sides measure 6 cm and 8 cm. What is the length of the remaining side, in centimeters?
author_rationale: The author had both 6 and 8 as the legs in mind, giving a hypotenuse of √(6² + 8²) = √100 = 10 cm.

intended_defect.description: The stem never says whether the 8 cm side is a leg or the hypotenuse, so the 'remaining side' is not determined. Two readings are both defensible for a right triangle with two known sides.
intended_defect.expected_finding: Reading A = both 6 and 8 are legs → remaining side is the hypotenuse = √(36 + 64) = √100 = 10. Reading B = 8 is the hypotenuse and 6 is a leg → remaining side is the other leg = √(64 − 36) = √28 ≈ 5.29. The two answers differ and both appear among the options, so the ambiguity attack is valid.
